memoryArtifacts.headline: The Garrett Family in England.pdf
memoryArtifacts.description: This extensive research appears to have been completed by John E. Garrett Jr. who also provided the Garrett Family Genealogy attached in several of the same records. Everyone in this document is not yet tagged. Link - https://www.yumpu.com/en/document/read/20936828/the-garretts-in-england-garrett-family-genealogy

popularStory.headline:
Sir Henry Neville of Latimer
(1437 – 26 July 1469)
popularStory.description:
Son and heir to Sir George de Neville and Elizabeth Beauchamp. 

Grandson of Sir Ralph de Neville and Joan de Beaufort, daughter of John of Gaunt, Sir Richard Beauchamp, Earl of Warwick, and Elizabeth …

zh.lifeSummary: 當Sir Henry Neville於1437年出生在英國巴金哈姆爾拉梅時，他的父親George Neville Lord Latimer22歲，母親Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp20歲。他於1462年與Lady Joan Bourchier在英國艾可哈爾斯亞德結婚。他們至少是7個兒子和1個女兒的父母。他去世於1469年7月26日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾班波瑞享年32歲，埋葬在英國英格蘭瓦維其可爾沃里克。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Sir Henry Neville于1437年出生在英国巴金哈姆爾拉梅时，他的父亲George Neville Lord Latimer22岁，母亲Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp20岁。他于1462年与Lady Joan Bourchier在英国艾可哈爾斯亞德结婚。他们至少是7个儿子和1个女儿的父母。他去世于1469年7月26日在英国英格兰奧可福爾班波瑞享年32岁，埋葬在英国英格兰瓦維其可爾沃里克。

nameMeanings.name: Neville
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English and Irish (of Norman origin): habitational name from Neuville in Calvados or Néville in Seine-Maritime, both so called from Old French neu(f) ‘new’ (from Latin novus) + ville ‘settlement’. One family line, originally from Neuville (Calvados), became powerful during the Wars of the Roses through Richard Neville (1428–71), 16th Earl of Warwick, nicknamed "The Kingmaker".
Irish (Munster): assimilation of the Gaelic name Ó Niadh (see Nee ) and sometimes of Ó Cnaimhín (see Nevin ).

History: George Neville came to VA in or c. 1700 and settled on the headwaters of the Occoquan River, acquiring a large estate. His descendants, bearing the surnames Neville and Craig, were of considerable importance in Pittsburgh, PA, and Cincinnati, OH.

meanings: English (of Norman origin): variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.

variants.name: Nevil

variants.name: Newill

variants.name: Nevill
meanings: English and Irish: of Norman origin, a variant of   Neville  .

variants.name: Nevels
meanings: English and Scottish (Wigtownshire): of Norman origin, a variant of Neville , with post-medieval excrescent -s. This surname is now rare in Britain.
Dutch and North German: unexplained.

variants.name: Nee
meanings: Some characteristic forenames:  Irish Dermot, John Patrick.

Irish (Galway): shortened Anglicized form of Gaelic Ó Niadh ‘descendant of Nia’ or Mac Niadh ‘son of Nia’. Compare McNee . Needham 2 and Neville have also been adopted as Anglicized forms of this name.
North German: possibly a relationship name from Middle Low German neve ‘nephew, grand-child’.

nameMeanings.name: Henry
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: A perennially popular given name, of Continental Germanic origin, from haim ‘home’ + rīc ‘power, ruler’. It was an Old French name, adopted by the Normans and introduced by them to Britain. It has been borne by eight kings of England. Not until the 17th century did the form Henry (as opposed to Harry ) become the standard vernacular form, mainly under the influence of the Latin form Henricus and French Henri.

historicEvents.title: Wars of the Roses
historicEvents.description: Wars of the Roses was a series of English civil wars for control of the throne of England between the royal families House of Lancaster and the House of York. The name of the war was derived as the red rose was the symbol of the Lancastrians and the white rose was the symbol of the Yorks.

en.lifeSummary: When Sir Henry Neville was born in 1437, in Latimer, Buckinghamshire, England, his father, George Neville Lord Latimer, was 22 and his mother, Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp, was 20. He married Lady Joan Bourchier in 1462, in Halstead, Essex, England. They were the parents of at least 7 sons and 1 daughter. He died on 26 July 1469, in Banbury, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 32, and was buried in Warwick, Warwickshire, England, United Kingdom.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Sir Henry Neville est né en 1437, à Latimer, Buckinghamshire, Angleterre, son père, George Neville Lord Latimer, avait 22 ans et sa mère, Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp, avait 20 ans. Il a épousé Lady Joan Bourchier en 1462, à Halstead, Essex, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 7 fils et 1 fille. Il est décédé le 26 juillet 1469, à Banbury, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 32 ans, et a été enterré à Warwick, Warwickshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni.

ja.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Nevilleが1437年にイギリスブクインガムシュアイアラティマーで生まれた時，父George Neville Lord Latimerは22歳，母Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchampは20歳であった。1462年にイギリスエセックスハルステアドでLady Joan Bourchierと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも7人の息子と1人の娘の親であった。彼は1469年7月26日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアバンブリーで32歳で死去し，イギリスイギリスワーウィクシュアイアワーウィックにて埋葬された。

ko.lifeSummary: Sir Henry Neville(이)가 1437년 영국 벅잉함실르 레이팀어에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 George Neville Lord Latimer(은)는 22세였고, 그의 어머니 Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp(은)는 20세였다. 그는 1462년 영국 에세스 할스테아드에서 Lady Joan Bourchier(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 일곱 명의 아들과 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그는 1469년 7월 26일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 반버이에서 32세의 나이로 사망했으며 영국 잉글랜드 워윅실르 워윅에 묻혔다.

Ele casou-se com Lady Joan Bourchier em 1462, em Halstead, Essex, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 7 filhos e 1 filha. ru.lifeSummary: Когда Sir Henry Neville родился в 1437 г. в местности латимир, буккингхамшир, Англия, его отцу по имени George Neville Lord Latimer было 22 года, а матери, которую звали Baroness Latimer Elizabeth De Beauchamp, 20. Он женился на женщине по имени Lady Joan Bourchier в 1462 г. в местности холстид, эссекс, Англия. У них было по крайней мере 7 сыновей и 1 дочь. Он умер 26 июля 1469 г. в местности банбури, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 32 лет и был похоронен в местности варвикк, варвиккшир, Англия, Великобритания.
